mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.5 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts household disposable income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.5, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate synchronized supply-side capacity expansion meeting robust aggregate demand, which lifts corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of rolling 12-month windows, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression fails to achieve statistical significance at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, stable consumer price inflation combined with near-zero policy rates anchors long-term yield curves, reducing equity term premiums and mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL remains flat and FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over any 18-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales figures directly reflect expanding consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days without requiring additional macroeconomic filters.
failure_condition: The monthly change in RSAFS shows a Spearman rank correlation below 0.05 with 5-day SPX returns across three consecutive calendar years, indicating the signal has lost predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index crosses above its 100-level baseline, it confirms that manufacturing capacity utilization has fully recovered, triggering a positive repricing of cyclical equities and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero in the 30 trading days following every INDPRO cross above 100, or the signal generates more false positives than actual positive return days over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 45% over any 24-month rolling window, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth paired with subdued inflation compresses the equity risk premium by confirming a soft-landing scenario, which mechanically lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns turn negative when GDPC1 quarterly growth exceeds 2.5% and CPIAUCSL annualized inflation remains below 2.0%, or the hypothesis fails to maintain a positive information coefficient over a 3-year out-of-sample test.
